2nd June, 2026 
 
Stock Code: BSE: 500696  
 
       NSE: HINDUNILVR  
      ISIN: INE030A01027 
 
Dear Sir/Madam, 
 
Sub: Integrated Annual Report for the financial year ended 31st March, 2026 
 
This is further to our letter dated 30th April, 2026 wherein we had informed that the Annual 
General Meeting (AGM) of the Company is scheduled to be held on Tuesday, 30th June, 2026 
through Video Conferencing/Other Audio Visual Means, in compliance with the provisions of the 
Companies Act, 2013 and the relevant circulars issued by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs in this 
regard.  
 
In terms of the requirements of Regulation 34(1) of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure 
Requirements) (Listing Regulations), we are submitting herewith the Integrated Annual Report of 
the Company including the Business Responsibility and Sustainability Report (BRSR) and the 
Notice of AGM for the financial year 2025-26, which is being sent through electronic mode to 
those Members whose e-mail addresses are registered with the Company/Registrar and 
Transfer Agent (RTA)/Depository Participants (DPs). Further, in accordance with Regulation 
36(1)(b) of the Listing Regulations, the Company is also sending a letter to those Members whose 
e-mail addresses are not registered with the Company/RTA/DPs, providing the weblink from 
where the Integrated Annual Report for the financial year 2025-26 can be accessed on the 
Company’s website. 
 
The Integrated Annual Report containing the BRSR and Notice of AGM is also uploaded on the 
website of the Company at https://www.hul.co.in/investors/annual-reports-and-performance-
highlights/annual-reports/ and on the website of NSDL at https://www.evoting.nsdl.com/.

Basis of Preparation and Presentation
This Integrated Annual Report of Hindustan Unilever 
Limited presents our financial and non-financial 
performance, strategic priorities, and value creation 
model. It aligns with global best practices, covering our 
business model, governance, stakeholder engagement, 
sustainability, and risk management. Designed for 
transparency and accountability, it meets the needs 
of investors, regulators, customers, employees, and 
communities. The terms ‘HUL’, ‘the Company’, ‘your 
Company’, ‘our Company’, ‘we’, ‘our’, and ‘us’ refer to 
Hindustan Unilever Limited.
Reporting Period
From 1st April, 2025 to 31st March, 2026 
(unless specifically mentioned otherwise).
Reporting Boundary
This Report follows the Integrated Reporting Framework, 
showcasing an interconnected view of our financial and 
non-financial performance, business model, six capitals, 
key strategic priorities, stakeholder engagement, risks and 
associated opportunities, and outcomes impacting value 
creation.
No significant changes in structure, ownership or reporting 
boundaries occurred during the reporting period, 
other than the demerger of the Ice Cream business. 
The results of the Ice Cream Business Undertaking for 
the previous and current financial years have been 
presented separately as ‘Discontinued Operations’, with 
the Statement of Profit and Loss bifurcated into Continuing 
and Discontinued Operations. The data remains consistent 
with the prior year, ensuring comparability and continuity 
in metrics and disclosures.
All the information presented in this Report pertains to 
consolidated operations of Hindustan Unilever Limited 
(unless specifically mentioned otherwise).
Accountability Statement
The Board and Management have reviewed this Integrated 
Annual Report to ensure it delivers a fair, balanced, 
and comprehensive view of our performance, business 
model, strategy, and material risks and opportunities. The 
Board confirms that the Report aligns with the Integrated 
Reporting Framework.
Materiality Determination
We assess material issues affecting our long-term value 
creation, integrating stakeholder insights, business 
priorities, and emerging risks. Aligned with European 
Sustainability Reporting Standards (ESRS), our materiality 
process ensures transparency and accountability. This 
Report presents balanced, objective information on key 
matters influencing HUL’s value creation journey.

Information
Internally reviewed and assured by the Company’s 
Management.

section_title: Corporate Overview
chunk:
Partnering India’s 
Growth Journey
HUL at a Glance
Hindustan Unilever Limited (HUL) is India’s largest FMCG 
company, serving consumers for over 90 years with a guiding 
belief that ‘What is good for India is good for HUL’. With a 
diversified portfolio and a robust distribution network, the 
Company reaches 9 out of 10 Indian households with one or 
more of its brands.
HUL places consumer needs at the core of its operations, 
continuously evolving to meet changing aspirations. 
The Company drives growth through science-backed 
innovations, leveraging deep R&D capabilities to develop 
products that deliver superior efficacy and relevance. Its 
innovation agenda is increasingly augmented by advanced 
analytics and artificial intelligence embedded across 
the value chain, from demand sensing and supply chain 
optimisation to precision marketing and personalised 
consumer engagement, enhancing both agility and 
effectiveness.
Sustainability remains integral to HUL’s strategy, with a 
strong focus on responsible sourcing, efficient resource 
utilisation, and reducing environmental impact. Together, 
its consumer-centric approach, innovation excellence, and 
technology-enabled capabilities position HUL to deliver 
sustainable, long-term value for all stakeholders.

Innovating for the new India 
with LLoTF
To drive premiumisation and design for the new India, we have invested 
in a New Advanced ‘Liquids Lab of the Future’ (LLoTF) in Mumbai. 
The LLoTF is designed to accelerate innovation across formats such as 
bodywash, shampoo, and laundry liquids, delivering superior products 
at scale while significantly reducing time-to-market. By prioritising 
AI and digital-led research and experimentation, this lab leverages 
advanced automation, robotics and data science to expedite 
experimentation, formulation and scale-up. 
Inaugurated in November 2025, this lab has been designed with a 
set of future-fit principles in mind: Targeted automation of repetitive 
and time-consuming activities and for 24x7 product analyses. It 
has capabilities for an enhanced understanding of product science 
and rapid prototyping at 6X scale of manual batch-making. The lab 
captures end-to-end data for AI-led predictive models and simulations 
resulting in zero ‘dark data’
The LLoTF also incorporates ‘phygital’ twins of scaled-down 
manufacturing operations that replicate factory equipment. This 
eliminates the need for factory trials and enables the prediction of 
scaleup challenges through advanced sensors and data models, 
accelerating production rollout.

section_title: Chairman's Statement
chunk:
Chairman's Statement
Our contribution extends well beyond the products 
we sell. Through our extensive distribution network, we 
enable livelihoods across the value chain—for distributors, 
retailers, suppliers, farmers and our Shakti entrepreneurs 
in rural India. Our operations support thousands of direct 
and indirect jobs, with a manufacturing footprint that 
spans the length and breadth of the country.
We are equally committed to ensuring that our growth is 
responsible. We have made strong progress on climate 
action, with 97% of our operations powered by renewable 
energy. Through the Hindustan Unilever Foundation, our 
not-for-profit subsidiary focused on addressing India’s 
water challenges, we have helped create cumulative 
water potential of over 4.5 trillion litres.
We continue to advance plastic circularity through 
initiatives such as Project Circular Bharat. Our Suvidha 
Centres—originally designed to provide accessible 
urban sanitation—have evolved into integrated Water, 
Sanitation and Hygiene (WASH) models. These centres 
now deliver safe water access, improved sanitation and 
hygiene services to over 600,000 people in Mumbai. This 
is responsible growth in action.
Building for the Future
Even as we contribute to India’s present, we are investing 
decisively in its future.
We are reimagining our business with the consumer at 
the centre—sharpening our portfolio, strengthening our 
route-to-market and embedding technology across 
our operations. Our strategic investments in digital-first, 
science-led brands reflect our ambition to build future 
growth engines in premium beauty, health and wellbeing.
Technology and data are central to this transformation. 
We are deploying advanced analytics and artificial 
intelligence across our value chain—from personalised 
consumer engagement to a more responsive, data-led 
supply chain.
We remain equally committed to building future-ready 
talent—underpinned by a culture of ownership, learning 
and adaptability. Together, these capabilities will power 
HUL’s long-term growth.
Good Governance
While we transform to meet the challenges of a 
changing world, we remain anchored in our values. 
We are committed to the highest standards of integrity 
and corporate governance in all that we do. We believe 
that long-term success is built on responsible business 
practices—towards our employees, partners, communities 
and the environment.
During the year, we announced the appointment of Priya 
Nair as CEO and Managing Director, succeeding Rohit 
Jawa. On behalf of the Board, I would like to thank Rohit 
for his leadership through a challenging period and for 
strengthening the foundations of the business. Under 
his stewardship, the Company took decisive actions to 
sharpen its portfolio and enhance competitiveness.
I am confident that Priya’s deep understanding of 
consumers, strong leadership and proven track record will 
accelerate HUL’s growth journey and drive the next phase 
of transformation.
Looking Ahead
The near-term environment will continue to present 
challenges. However, India has consistently demonstrated 
resilience, emerging stronger through periods of 
uncertainty.
For HUL, with deep roots in the country, we will navigate 
this environment with agility and discipline—anchored in 
our unwavering commitment to serve our consumers.
Our brands, trusted by millions, remain our greatest 
strength. Our people are our enduring competitive 
advantage. And our belief in India’s growth story continues 
to guide every decision we make.
I would like to express my sincere appreciation to our 
employees, partners and customers for their continued 
support. And to you, our shareholders—thank you for your 
trust and confidence in our journey.
Together, we will continue to build a future-fit HUL, aligned 
to the aspirations of a rising India.
Warm Regards, 
Nitin Paranjpe  
Chairman, 
Hindustan Unilever Limited
While India is navigating near-term 
headwinds, the core drivers of its long-
term growth—strong domestic demand, 
structural reforms and a resilient 
economic foundation—remain intact.
A Nation on the Rise
India’s economic progress in recent years has been 
encouraging. Our GDP continues to grow among the 
fastest globally, supported by structural reforms and a 
policy environment that fosters investment and enterprise.
Three structural drivers underpin this momentum. First, 
India’s young and expanding population continues to fuel 
both consumption and innovation. Second, per capita 
consumption—while steadily rising—remains well below 
global benchmarks, indicating significant headroom for 
growth across categories. Across Home Care, Beauty 
& Wellbeing, Personal Care, Foods, millions of Indian 
consumers are only beginning their journey towards 
premiumisation.
Third, India’s digital transformation has been truly 
transformative. With over 800 million internet users, 
widespread smartphone adoption and a robust digital 
payments infrastructure, India is now among the most 
connected economies in the world. This digital backbone 
is reshaping how consumers discover, evaluate and 
purchase products, while also enabling a new generation 
of entrepreneurs to scale rapidly.
Alongside this, progressive policy measures and sustained 
investments in infrastructure continue to strengthen the 
foundation for growth. The rise of startups, expansion of 
organised retail and the rapid growth of quick commerce 
all reflect a nation that is ambitious, innovative and 
increasingly confident of its place in the world.
Hindustan Unilever – Partnering the Nation
Hindustan Unilever has always believed that the progress 
of India and HUL are deeply intertwined. Today, this 
belief guides us more than ever. Our brands reach 9 out 
of 10 Indian households, serving consumers across price 
points—from essential products to premium offerings. 
Whether it is a ₹1 sachet in a rural kirana store or a 
premium product on a quick commerce platform, our 
endeavour is to make everyday living better for every 
Indian.
Dear Shareholders,
The financial year 2025–26 tested 
the adaptability and resilience of 
businesses. Geopolitical tensions, 
commodity volatility and rapid 
technological shifts continued to 
reshape operating environments 
across the world.
Performance Overview

section_title: CEO & MD’s Statement
chunk:
CEO & MD’s Statement
Stepped-up Performance during  
the Year
Financial Year 2026 saw an improvement in the demand 
environment, supported by a favourable macro-economic 
backdrop. A series of fiscal policy measures by the 
Government, such as GST and income tax reforms, 
supportive monetary actions, and lower headline inflation 
helped ease pressure on household budgets, creating an 
environment for consumption and demand recovery. At the 
same time, the cost landscape remained volatile, impacted 
by sharp commodity movements, and currency headwinds.
Against this backdrop, we delivered a turnover of ₹63,763 
crores, growing 5% with 4% Underlying Volume Growth 
(UVG). More importantly, we stepped up our performance 
sequentially from 3% Underlying Sales Growth (USG) in first 
half of 2026, to 5% in December quarter and to 7% in March 
quarter. Growth was broad-based during the year, with all 
segments contributing, and we further strengthened our 
market leadership through the year.
EBITDA Margin stood at 23.6%, and Profit After Tax from 
continuing business was ₹10,652 crores. The Board of 
Directors proposed a final dividend of ₹22 per share, 
subject to shareholder approval at the AGM. Together with 
the interim dividend of ₹19 per share declared in October 
2025, the total payout for the year stands at ₹9,633 crores—
reflecting our continued commitment to delivering strong 
and consistent shareholder returns.
Strategic Priorities to Unlock Growth
Operating successfully in this environment required a 
careful balance of discipline in making clear portfolio 
choices, allocating capital prudently, and agility in 
execution. We crafted sharper priorities during the year, 
with a clear focus on volume-led revenue growth. 
First, we deepened our consumer segmentation approach—
making it central to how we design propositions, shape 
markets, and win across channels. This is embedded in 
every decision we make, whether it’s product, proposition, 
pricing, or the channel we use to reach and persuade 
consumers. 
Second, we invested in building more desirable brands 
- premium, purpose-led and culturally relevant. This was 
supported by a robust pipeline of science-led, 
consumer-backed innovations and amplified through 
digital-first demand generation. 
Third, we accelerated future-proofing of our frontline by 
strengthening our footprint in specialised general trade, 
and leveraging partnerships and technology to win in 
channels of the future.
Finally, we amplified focus on resource allocation in fewer, 
bigger bets in high-growth demand spaces. We believe 
we have a right to win in these spaces by deploying a 
combination of curated innovation, market development, 
and brand elevation.
In line with our strategic priorities, this year, we announced 
a proposed investment of up to ₹2,000 crores to expand 
manufacturing capacity in fast-growing premium 
categories across Beauty & Wellbeing and Home Care 
liquids. 
We also simplified the organisation to enhance speed and 
agility. A unified India structure, the introduction of a Chief 
Marketing Officer and a Chief R&D Officer roles, along with 
a dedicated India R&D function, collectively compressed 
decision cycles and enabled sharper, faster execution.
Winning with Brands
Our brands remain at the heart of our success, serving 
diverse needs of our consumers across price points and 
occasions. 
Home Care delivered another year of strong volume 
growth, driven by sustained demand and sharp execution. 
We maintained leadership across segments and continued 
on our premiumisation agenda with strong value offerings 
across price tiers through our liquids portfolio. 
Innovation-led market development actions included 
actions in Surf excel Matic liquids, Comfort fabric 
conditioners and Vim dishwash liquid.
In Beauty & Wellbeing, we delivered healthy growth, led 
by Hair Care, Premium Skin Care and Health & Wellbeing. 
During the year, we focused on sharpening our portfolio to 
reflect the needs and aspirations of the new India through 
strategic investments in Minimalist and OZiva. Minimalist 
is now expanding offline and unlocking synergies across 
media, manufacturing and procurement while OZiva 
continues to scale, supported by a robust science-backed 
innovation portfolio.
Personal Care delivered steady growth, with strong 
momentum in premium segments of skin cleansing, 
bodywash and deodorants. The portfolio continued to 
evolve towards premium, science-backed and sensorial 
formats, with key innovations in brands such as Dove, 
Lux and Closeup. Bodywash continued to scale rapidly, 
reflecting structural shifts in consumer preferences and 
everyday routines.
Foods delivered a step-up in performance supported by 
strong momentum in Coffee and Lifestyle Nutrition. The 
business continued to expand into high-growth demand 
spaces through new launches in Horlicks and Boost, 
portfolio expansion in Kissan and geographic expansion in 
Beverages. Strong growth in Channels of the Future aided in 
delivering a mid-single digit UVG for the segment.
AI and Digital
We are accelerating our investments in technology and AI 
as a core enabler of growth. AI is helping us understand 
consumers better and engage with them in more 
meaningful, personalised ways at scale.
AI and advanced analytics are transforming our supply 
chain—making it more responsive, more agile and more 
resilient. Initiatives such as digital twins, advanced analytics 
and our supply chain nerve centre are helping us build 
an increasingly intelligent, interconnected manufacturing 
network. Today, five of our factories have been recognised 
as Advanced Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Lighthouses 
by the World Economic Forum.
Doing Well by Doing Good
Sustainability remains integral to the business. We have 
made strong progress on climate action with 16 sites now 
operating on 100% renewable energy.
We continue to scale regenerative agriculture, now 
covering over 61,000 hectares. On plastics, we continued 
efforts to reduce, redesign and recycle packaging. Through 
our livelihoods initiatives, we continued to support farmers, 
suppliers and Shakti entrepreneurs. 
A Future-fit Workplace
Our people are central to our success. We are investing in 
building a future-ready, high-performing organisation—
through focused upskilling, external partnerships and 
AI-enabled tools that support our frontline and shop-floor 
teams.
We are equally committed to fostering an inclusive culture 
anchored in trust and belonging, and to advancing diversity 
through initiatives such as Womankind, Enable and ProUd.
Looking Ahead
Financial Year 2026 has been a year of meaningful progress, 
during which we not only delivered stronger performance 
but also reinforced the foundations of our business. 
While the external environment remains dynamic, we 
enter the next year with sharper strategic clarity, stronger 
fundamentals, and a clear conviction in the direction we 
are taking. We are confident that our priorities and business 
fundamentals position us well to power the nation on the 
rise. As always, our core belief continues to guide us:  
what is good for India, is good for HUL.
I thank every HUL employee for their passion and 
commitment, and our partners, suppliers and customers for 
their continued support.
I also thank our shareholders for their trust and confidence 
as we shape the next chapter of growth together.
Warm regards,
Priya Nair 
Chief Executive Officer & Managing Director 
Hindustan Unilever Limited
Dear Shareholders,
It is an honour to write to you for the first 
time as the Chief Executive Officer and 
Managing Director of Hindustan Unilever 
Limited. I step into this role with deep 
pride in HUL’s legacy, and a strong belief 
in the India opportunity. 
As India’s leading FMCG company, our focus is clear - to 
deliver competitive, volume-led revenue growth. What gives 
me the confidence to achieve this is the strength of our brands, 
resilience of our business model, and the trust of millions of 
Indian consumers who use our products every day.
Performance Overview

section_title: Performance Overview - Home Care
chunk:
Review of the Year
The Home Care business delivered another year of 
high-single digit Underlying Volume Growth (UVG) reflecting 
sustained consumer demand across categories. Price 
reductions, implemented against a backdrop of deflationary 
commodities in the earlier part of the year while maintaining 
a competitive price-value proposition, led to moderation in 
Underlying Sales Growth (USG) to 4%. 
The operating environment was characterised by rapid 
format upgradation to liquids driven by calibrated pricing 
actions to sharpen access and affordability. 
The business invested in amplifying market making to 
drive trials and disproportionate penetration gains in 
Liquids across all tiers leading to strong double-digit 
UVG in the year. These actions were complemented 
by sharper channel design, partnerships and scaled 
demand generation, enabling the business to build value 
and further strengthen its leadership position through 
consistent gains in volume share.
Winning Across the Demographic Pyramid
Our Home Care portfolio is designed to meet the diverse needs of Indian households, ensuring tailored 
solutions for every segment. By spanning mass, popular, and premium segments, our portfolio ensures broad 
accessibility, addresses varied functional and economic needs and anticipates changing aspirations. This 
comprehensive approach allows us to remain relevant and trusted partners in homes across India.
Home Care
Home Care’s growth strategy is rooted in building category 
value through format upgradation and premiumisation. As 
consumer expectations continue to evolve, we are shaping 
the transition with a future-fit portfolio and capabilities 
that strengthen brand love and marketing effectiveness 
across emerging media and sales channels. Amid supply 
chain volatility and commodity cycles, we will maintain a 
disciplined price–value framework, ensuring affordability 
while protecting the long-term business health. This balance 
underpins our ability to deliver consistent, competitive and 
profitable growth over time. 
Vandana Suri 
Executive Director, Home Care
Wheel offers affordable, 
effective cleaning for the 
mass market
Sun liquid addresses 
essential dishwashing 
needs at accessible 
prices
Rin and Sunlight provide 
reliable stain removal for 
value-seeking families
Vim’s range, available in bar 
and liquid formats, offers 
advanced cleaning and 
superior benefits for evolving 
household preferences
Premium brands like Comfort and 
Surf excel deliver superior performance 
for enhanced convenience and a better 
washing experience

HINDUSTAN UNILEVER LIMITED
20


Creating desirable, SASSY brands
Doubling-down on Fewer,  
Bigger Bets
Structural shifts in Indian households continue to reshape 
the Home Care landscape, creating new opportunities 
for category expansion and value creation. As consumers 
progressively upgrade formats and usage occasions 
diversify, there is a clear opportunity to create and shape 
new segments through sustained market development. 
Building on these shifts, the business continued to invest 
behind market-making innovations designed to unlock new 
demand spaces, accelerate format upgradation and elevate 
category value.
Vim 
Social-first 
Engagement
Vim elevated its social-first engagement, 
taking the brand to new frontiers across digital 
platforms. Collaborations with Everything 
Food, integrations with popular shows such as 
MasterChef, and partnerships with celebrity 
influencers including Farah Khan through her 
home-cooking content enabled the brand to 
further embed itself in contemporary food and 
cooking culture. By adding its own distinctive 
twist to social media trends, Vim strengthened 
cultural relevance and amplified its superiority 
credentials, reinforcing its leadership in 
dishwashing liquids.
Introducing Vim ProClean Liquids with RhamnoTech
In Household Care, Vim ProClean liquids reinforced Vim’s leadership in upgrading the dishwashing category. 
Enhanced foam, improved fragrance, and natural actives powered by our proprietary RhamnoTech biosurfactant 
technology offer benefits like soft-on-hands care, anti-bacterial assurance, and superior sensorial experience, 
driving adoption of premium liquids. 
Surf excel Playing Beyond 
the Boundary
Extending beyond broadcast, Surf excel partnered with 
widely followed franchises in Indian Premier League 
(IPL), taking the sporting fervour to trade partners 
and consumers alike, and strengthening brand love 
through on ground and digital touch-points. The brand 
further amplified its social relevance through ‘moment 
marketing’ - by turning moments from real sporting 
achievements into conversation drivers—most notably 
when it playfully engaged with Indian women’s team 
cricketer Jemimah Rodrigues, urging her to frame, 
not wash, her muddy jersey following a splendid 
performance in ICC Women’s World Cup tournament. 
Surf excel Social-
first Engagement
Surf excel exemplified the Company’s Social-first 
Demand Generation approach by anchoring its ‘Play 
On’ platform in the country’s most watched sporting 
moments. During the cricket season, the brand took over 
screens with a series of quirky, contextually timed films 
that brought alive a familiar Indian truth—food stains 
picked up in the excitement of watching the game.
Wheel’s  
WiMI Approach
Wheel, our mass-market laundry brand, refreshed its 
communication to modernise the brand, emphasising 
excellent cleaning and appealing fragrance. The 
narrative, rooted in Indian households, reflects evolving 
relationships in a relatable manner. Wheel’s superior 
formulation, shaped by the Winning in Many Indias 
(WiMI) approach, addresses regional wash habits and 
expectations. Combining functional excellence with 
culturally resonant storytelling, Wheel has strengthened 
brand salience and trust, emerging as India’s No. 1 mass 
laundry powder detergent brand by volume this year. 
Future-proofing 
Frontline Engine 
Shopping behaviour in Home Care continues to evolve 
with rapid expansion of modern trade and speciality 
channels such as open-format stores which are 
increasingly preferred for discovery-led and planned 
purchases. In parallel, quick commerce has emerged as 
a fast-growing channel, catering to 
immediacy-driven needs through rapid, on-demand 
deliveries, and reshaping expectations around availability 
and convenience.
Our evolved portfolio strategically incorporates targeted 
innovations designed to address niche and emerging 
consumer demands, establishing a strong and relevant 
presence across e-commerce, quick commerce and 
modern trade. These efforts were complemented by 
collaborative category initiatives and curated events 
with key channel partners, strengthening customer 
partnerships and enabling more effective tracking of 
shoppers across their omni-channel purchase journey. 
Vim Floor Cleaners and Comfort Perfume Deluxe range 
that were launched in these channels have captured 
a significant share in a short duration, showcasing our 
partnership and executional strengths. 
Surf excel, India’s leading 
Fabric Wash brand with 
turnover of over ₹11,000 
crores, has strengthened 
its leadership in premium 
powders through exceptional 
product performance and 
deep consumer trust. 
The Home Care category remains well positioned for 
sustained growth, driven by ongoing format upgradation, 
premiumisation and rising consumer expectations for 
performance and convenience. Structural tailwinds—
urbanisation, higher laundry frequency, and increased 
adoption of liquids—continue to expand category value. 
With strong brands, superior products and a proven 
market development playbook, we are poised to lead this 
evolution, unlock new segments, and deliver consistent, 
competitive growth while shaping the future of Home 
Care in India.
Outlook
Positioned as a value offering, it continued to scale strongly 
with its clear promise of ‘Bright like New’.
Together, these brands enable a clear play across the value 
pyramid in liquids, addressing varied consumer needs and 
affordability thresholds. 
Vim continued to expand its presence as a Household Care 
Masterbrand, with its floor cleaner range further scaling and 
strengthening its market presence following last year’s launch. 
Comfort, a ₹1,000 crore+ brand, continued to lead the fabric 
conditioners market. Comfort Perfume Deluxe, Comfort’s 
premium range expanded the brand’s presence in premium 
fragrance-led demand spaces. Drawing inspiration from 
luxury perfumery, the range elevates the fabric care 
experience by delivering long-lasting and indulgent 
fragrance, addressing the growing consumer desire for 
sensorial differentiation and premiumisation.
In liquids, Surf excel Matic Express was introduced to address 
evolving consumer expectations around speed, convenience 
and superior performance in machine wash. Designed for 
contemporary washing habits, the launch strengthens Surf 
excel’s leadership in advanced formats while expanding the 
relevance of liquids among time-constrained households. 
Complementing this, Surf excel Smart Shots continued to 
scale as a single-dose, soluble capsule format, catering to 
consumers seeking precision, ease of use and premium 
experiences, while reinforcing the brand’s leadership in 
next-generation laundry solutions.
Rin liquids continued to play a critical role in democratising 
liquid adoption and expanding the format across price tiers. 
We accelerated upgradation to premium powders 
with the introduction of a monthly pack at an 
accessible price of J99. Launched in India’s 
central belt, it targets value-conscious consumers 
where mass powders dominate, enabling 
premiumisation. This strategy encourages trial and 
gradual transition to superior, performance-driven 
laundry solutions.

section_title: Beauty & Wellbeing - Performance Overview
chunk:
In FY 2025–26, Beauty & Wellbeing (B&W) delivered 
Underlying Sales Growth of 6%, driven by a balanced mix 
of volume and price. As thought leaders in the segment, 
we continued to influence category direction across Hair 
Care, Health & Wellbeing, Skin Care and Colour Cosmetics, 
shaping trends through superior innovation, deep 
consumer insight and distinctive execution.
Hair Care, Health & Wellbeing and Premium Skin 
Care delivered strong growth, reflecting the impact 
of our strategic choices to create desire at scale. We 
strengthened our Health & Wellbeing play through OZiva, 
democratised access across price tiers, and unlocked new 
demand through social-first content and influencer-led 
Review of the Year
engagement. Mass Skin Care remained subdued, in line 
with shifting consumer preferences and category  
re-evaluation.
However, Channels of the Future delivered competitive 
double-digit growth in Skin Care in an increasingly 
dynamic landscape, underscoring the strength of HUL’s 
enterprise-wide strategy to build future-fit, channel

chunk:
Review of the Year
In FY 2025-26, Personal Care continued its journey of 
premiumisation with Underlying Sales Growth of 4% led by 
price increases taken on the back of sustained commodity 
inflation in palm and palm-based derivatives. Skin 
Cleansing achieved mid-single digit growth, underpinned 
by double-digit volume led growth in premium bars 
and bodywash. Oral Care delivered mid-single digit 
growth, anchored by Closeup’s freshness and whitening 
innovations, while Pepsodent reinforced therapeutic 
relevance. Deodorants saw significant portfolio evolution 
centred on premiumisation through roll‑ons and Dove 
sprays. Together, these segments signalled sustained 
improvement and firmly positioned Personal Care as a 
steady contributor to the portfolio.
During the year, the business sharpened its focus on 
premiumisation, building desirable brands, and strong 
execution in Channels of the Future. The business 
delivered a double-digit growth in organised trade. 
The portfolio continued to reshape through market 
development in bodywash, premiumising through brands 
like Dove, Pears and Closeup. This was reflected in the 
strengthened performance through high quality mix 
management and enhanced channel capabilities.
India’s Personal Care market is being reshaped by three consumer cohorts, Democratisers, Premiumisers and Power 
spenders, each unlocking distinct opportunities. Through focused innovation and channel expansion, Personal 
Care meets evolving consumer needs and pioneers newer experiences. This positions us to sustain growth and lead 
premiumisation, setting industry benchmarks for quality and desirability.
Our portfolio is crafted to serve all demographic segments and foster new market opportunities, keeping our brands 
relevant and aspirational.
Winning Across the Demographic Pyramid
For Democratisers, the priority is 
reach and affordability, ensuring 
recruitment through minis, singles, 
and larger packs that expand 
penetration in fast‑growing rural 
markets. 
Power Spenders demand

bodywash, and Rexona roll‑ons 
where experimentation and 
lifestyle adoption drive category 
development. 
Premiumisers accelerate innovation 
uptake, seeking advanced benefits 
like whitening, freshness, hydration, 
and skinification, which fuels trials in a 
format like bodywash. 
Personal Care
Personal Care continued to make steady progress amid evolving 
consumer needs and a changing operating environment. The 
business focused on strengthening its portfolio through focused 
market development, premiumisation, and improved execution 
across channels, while maintaining a balance between affordability 
and aspiration. This helped build relevance across the consumption 
pyramid, supporting both scale and trade up opportunities.

of our team, whose relentless consumer focus and execution

culture. Innovations like Dove’s serum bars, Lux’s vitamin formats, Pears’ glycerine reset, and Closeup’s whitening 
technology are not just launched, they are translated into snackable, mobile first stories that spark curiosity and travel 
organically. Campaigns are designed to be scroll stopping, with aesthetics dialled up across packaging, digital assets, and

Social-first 
Demand 
Generation

robust launchpad for future growth and enhanced results. 
By concentrating resources on these levers, Personal Care 
is sharpening brand equities, accelerating premiumisation, 
and capturing emerging consumer aspirations across 
urban and rural markets. This outlook reflects a deliberate 
pivot towards science-backed innovation, modern 
channel execution, and Social-first Demand Generation, 
positioning the business to deliver resilient growth and 
long-term shareholder value while shaping the future of 
the category.
Future-proofing 
Frontline Engine 
In FY 2025-26, performance highlighted strong 
rural expansion in general trade, premiumisation 
momentum in modern trade, and rapid acceleration 
in e-commerce and quick commerce. Each channel 
is being leveraged to its strengths, ensuring portfolio 
relevance and scale. 
General trade remains the backbone, particularly 
in rural markets. Premium formats are leading 
the upgrades, with large packs and minis driving 
penetration. Lifebuoy and Lux are building salience 
through rural digital activations and Pears is

Distribution expansion, sharp pricing actions, and

momentum. 
Modern trade has become the premiumisation 
engine, with investments in visibility, planogram

theatres. Dove, Lux, and Pears are leading growth, with

E-commerce and quick commerce are reshaping 
Personal Care through event-led, high-intent

Review of the Year
FY 2025-26 marked a year of shifting gears and 
purposeful momentum for the Foods category, supported 
by continued portfolio transformation and focused 
investments in high‑growth demand spaces. Overall, the 
business delivered 5% USG driven by mid-single digit 
UVG, with a meaningful performance step-up in each 
quarter. Growth was broad-based across Beverages, 
Lifestyle Nutrition and Packaged Foods, reflecting the 
impact of strategic portfolio interventions, innovation 
investments, strengthened channel capabilities and 
executional excellence.
Tea sustained market leadership, while Coffee delivered 
strong double-digit growth driven by pricing and 
geographic expansion. Packaged Foods continued its 
growth trajectory through market development actions, 
and Lifestyle Nutrition recorded four consecutive quarters 
of positive Underlying Volume Growth, signalling a 
turnaround in performance.
Winning Across the Demographic Pyramid
Our Foods business is uniquely positioned with a comprehensive portfolio, ready to win across India’s evolving 
demographics. The strength lies in our iconic brands, each catering to the diverse tastes and aspirations of contemporary 
Indian consumers. 
We strategically serve differentiated segments such as Power Spenders, Premiumisers, and Democratisers, by aligning 
brand investment, channel resources and innovation across our portfolio. This approach enables us to adapt and 
lead amidst India’s changing consumption patterns, ensuring our business remains relevant and resilient as consumer 
preferences shift towards convenience, health and premium experiences.
Foods
This year, we steered a step-up in our Foods business. 
We reignited brand love through purposeful marketing, 
sharpened our innovation engine and drove discipline in 
market execution to return to a strong growth trajectory. We

Outlook
Horlicks Pro Fitness is a science-led meal replacement 
shake for weight management to help keep the body 
nourished while cutting calories. These innovations elevate 
Horlicks into a holistic, convenient, Lifestyle Nutrition brand 
for modern households.
Looking ahead, the Foods business is well‑positioned to benefit 
from an improving macro‑economic environment and  
structural demand tailwinds reshaping consumption in India. 
The FY 2025-26 foundations-a pivot to Lifestyle Nutrition, 
a volume‑led Beverages business, an innovation‑driven 
Packaged Foods portfolio, strengthened Channels of the 
Future and Social‑first Demand Generation, enable accelerated 
performance. The business remains committed to consumer 
trust, scientific rigour and market development to deliver 
sustained, competitive and responsible growth for all its 
stakeholders.
The Foods business benefited from a growing consumer 
base discovering and repurchasing premium products 
on digital platforms. Accordingly, the Company made 
material advances in Channels of the Future in  
FY 2025-26, with e-commerce and quick commerce 
delivering strong double-digit growth. Quick commerce 
emerged as a strategically critical channel, expanding 
consumption occasions and accelerating discovery of 
products, formats and categories. It supported market 
development of Premium Beverages such as Bru Gold, 
Taj Mahal and Lipton, and scaled launches including 
Red Label Instant Tea, Kissan Chutneys and Hellmann’s 
Mayo variants. Relationships with modern trade and 
e-commerce further strengthened through deeper joint 
business planning, enhanced co-creation partnerships 
for Kissan Chutneys and Red Label Instant Tea, and a 
more segmented, channel-specific portfolio strategy.
Hellmann’s, the world’s No.1 mayonnaise brand, 
introduced its range of flavoured mayonnaises in Smoky 
Tandoori, Roasted Garlic and Cheezy Jalapeno Burst, 
driving strong growth led by organised trade. Under 
Knorr, International Cooking Aids were strengthened 
with Thai Red and Green Curry Pastes, tapping into 
quick commerce channel to deliver restaurant‑like taste 
at home. Our Unilever Food Solutions (UFS) business 
focused on brilliant basics of product superiority, 
portfolio differentiation and execution rigour to deliver a 
strong performance.
Future-proofing 
Frontline Engine 
Kissan is one of India’s most trusted food brands, deeply 
rooted in the country’s agricultural ecosystem and built 
on a long-standing association with Indian farmers and 
farm sourced produce. The Packaged Foods segment saw 
Kissan expand into the large Chutneys category with four 
variants, converting frequent scratch-cooking moments 
into branded, packaged options. Leveraging its trusted 
ketchup equity, Kissan brought quality and reliability to 
chutneys with silbatta-like texture and farm-sourced 
ingredients, modernising the condiment space. 
Nano DC Initiative – Powering HUL’s 
Next-generation Supply Chain
The Nano DC initiative aligns HUL’s supply ecosystem with evolving customer 
demand as consumer behaviour shifts towards faster, more dynamic and niche 
ordering patterns, where traditional replenishment models are no longer 
sufficient.
Designed to enable agile, high-frequency supply with zero disruption, Nano 
DCs support rapid growth across quick commerce, marketplaces and emerging 
customer segments through consistent availability across platforms.
The initiative also reflects a strategic shift from traditional volume-led Pareto 
models towards a more responsive growth-cohort-led supply approach, 
supporting HUL’s expanding portfolio of new-age brands and premium products 
operating at lower volumes but higher value density.
Nano DCs operate as channel-focused units within larger distribution centres, 
enabled by dedicated teams and docks, always-on operations, multimodal 
logistics capabilities, hardware readiness and dynamic transportation planning. 
HUL currently operates two Nano DCs, with four more planned this year.
Dedicated supervisors, customer-level tracking, specialised docks, Nano trucks, 
RFID tracking and GPS-enabled e-locking strengthen execution. Nano DCs 
flexibly service loads from 100 kgs to 15 tonnes and manage festive demand 
spikes annually while improving service reliability across channels.

Governance Overview
Governance Philosophy
Governance Structure
Our Corporate Governance philosophy is anchored in a rich legacy of over nine decades in India and has continuously 
evolved alongside our growth and the changing business landscape. At the heart of our approach are our core values of 
Integrity, Respect, Responsibility and Pioneering—values that define who we are and guide every aspect of our conduct. 
We view governance not merely as a matter of compliance, but as a fundamental way of doing business, rooted in 
transparency, accountability and ethical stewardship. Our actions are driven by a clear sense of purpose and a  
long-standing belief that business must serve a purpose beyond profit, creating sustainable value for all our 
stakeholders. This enduring commitment to principled conduct forms the bedrock of our corporate behaviour and 
continues to shape our journey of responsible and values-led growth.
The Company’s governance framework is multi-tiered, comprising the Board of Directors, its Committees, the CEO & MD, 
and the Management Committee. The Board remains committed to the highest standards of Corporate Governance, 
consistently going beyond statutory requirements in several areas. It retains ultimate responsibility for strategic direction, 
material investments and divestments, capital allocation and structure, as well as oversight of policies, internal controls, 
and risk management. The Board also plays a key role in shaping organisational culture and promoting ethical conduct.
While certain responsibilities are discharged directly, others are delegated to its five Committees to enable focused 
oversight. The Board is supported in the discharge of its responsibilities by the Committees, the CEO & MD, and the 
Management Committee.

section_title: Sustainability at HUL
chunk:
Materiality That Guides Action 
Material Topics
HUL’s Double Materiality covers both impact and financial 
perspectives. ‘Impact Materiality’ provides an ‘inside out’ 
view, focusing on the actual or potential short-, medium- 
and long-term impacts of our operations and value chain 
on people and the environment. 
On the other hand, ‘Financial Materiality’ offers an ‘outside in’ 
lens, assessing how sustainability-related risks and opportunities 
may influence the Company’s financial performance and 
position over the short-, medium- and long-term. 
Adopting Double Materiality Assessment (DMA) is a natural 
progression towards strengthening our sustainability 
strategy. It enables us to drive focused, meaningful action, 
while ensuring the business remains future-ready and 
aligned with evolving stakeholder expectations. Our 
materiality assessment helps identify priority sustainability 
issues across our operations and value chain, allowing 
us to address and report on what matters most to our 
stakeholders.

section_title: Research & Development (R&D) and Consumer Innovation
chunk:
Consumers
Research & Development (R&D) serves as a cornerstone 
of HUL’s competitive edge, combining world-class 
scientific expertise with deep, local consumer 
understanding to create products that truly resonate 
with the aspirations of Indian consumers. HUL benefits 
immensely from Unilever’s global R&D network and 
India R&D is a crucial part of this worldwide innovation 
ecosystem. During the year, the Unilever R&D organisation 
established an India-focused R&D Category Design & 
Deploy unit. This initiative significantly enhances our 
capacity to accelerate science-led, consumer-centric 
The R&D strategy is guided by a clear ambition: ‘To 
innovate boldly for people and planet while fulfilling the 
aspirations of consumers’. The company has strategically 
focused on the three next-generation science 
platforms—Microbiome & Biotics, Next-generation 
Materials, and Biotechnology, as key drivers of long-term 
growth. These multi-year research platforms are already 
powering breakthrough innovations across multiple 
categories, resulting in significant improvements in 
efficacy, sensory appeal, packaging, fragrances, flavours 
and other benefits that consumers can see and feel.
Discovering Emerging Needs 
through Sharp Consumer Insight
Designing Superior Solutions 
through Science & Innovation
innovations, closer to consumers. It strengthens local 
design and deployment capabilities, while continuing 
to harness the best of global science, technology, and 
innovations to benefit the Indian market. 
HUL’s R&D agenda has evolved to keep pace with India’s 
changing landscape, from developing solutions for a 
growing market, to pioneering the ‘Winning in Many Indias’ 
framework, and now driving innovation for a modern India 
through the SASSY framework. 
HUL’s R&D employs a wide range of physical and 
digital consumer insight tools, large-scale analytics, 
and rapid prototyping techniques to stay ahead 
of evolving tastes, routines and aspirations. This 
includes large‑scale digital analytics, always‑on 
virtual product tests, in‑person interactions and pilots 
that surface emerging needs, latent motivations and 
cultural nuances. The Agile Innovation Hub, powered 
by generative AI, enables R&D to identify emerging 
consumer trends early, translate them into product 
designs, and test them swiftly.

processes shifting online. HUL is leading this evolution by 
fostering seamless brand discovery, trial, and advocacy 
through a comprehensive, digital-first strategy. We are

and offers according to individual preferences, ensuring

management to nurture advocacy, empowering satisfied 
consumers to share their stories and become brand 
ambassadors. By orchestrating discovery, engagement, 
and delight across every stage, HUL drives frictionless 
brand interactions and builds lasting relationships, setting 
new benchmarks for excellence in India’s rapidly evolving 
market landscape. This holistic approach ensures our 
brands remain accessible, relevant, and trusted as we 
shape the future of consumer engagement. 
Strengthening Discovery, 
Engagement and Delight Across  
the Consumer Journey
As market leaders, our commitment extends beyond 
simply responding to current consumer needs. We are 
dedicated to actively shaping and developing entire 
categories by anticipating and nurturing new consumption 
occasions. This proactive approach is evident in our success 
stories within Home Care liquids, Fabric Conditioners and 
Hair Conditioners, where we have pioneered category 
growth and set benchmarks for excellence. Today, we are 
intensifying our efforts in high-growth segments such as 
Bodywash, Premium Skin and Hair Care, and Condiments, 
channelling our resources and expertise to accelerate 
their expansion and create new routines and occasions for 
consumers.
Leveraging our extensive distribution network and robust 
sampling programmes, we ensure that these innovations 
are not limited to a select few but democratised and 
made accessible to consumers across the nation. Our retail 
presence spanning both urban and rural markets enables 
us to bring these products to a wide audience, fostering 
inclusivity and driving adoption at scale. 
India is rapidly embracing an omni-channel shopping 
paradigm, where consumers expect to access their 
favourite brands instantly and seamlessly across every retail 
touchpoint. In this context, HUL has taken decisive actions 
across all channels to lead this transformation and deliver a 
truly omni-channel experience.

developing tailored route-to-market systems to accelerate

efficiency and reach while ensuring HUL’s brands remain 
accessible and relevant across the diverse and evolving retail 
landscape.

rapidly transforming India, while building strong moats

Customised Route-to-Market (RTM): 
Speciality Channels
To better serve diverse consumer needs, we are shifting 
from a one-size-fits-all approach to a nuanced, customised 
RTM strategy. By investing in dedicated infrastructure for 
Speciality Channels, we are unlocking growth frontiers 
that demand high expertise and specific shopping 
environments.
A primary pillar is the aggressive scaling of our coverage 
to activated chemist and cosmetic stores. These are 
critical touch-points for our Lifestyle Nutrition and Beauty 
& Wellbeing portfolios. To win, we are increasing physical 
reach while dialling up in-store visibility and medical 
marketing. Specialised teams now engage healthcare 
professionals and beauty experts to drive ‘expert-led’ 
demand, ensuring high off-take and long-term brand 
credibility. 
Customers
The expansion of 
Shikhar, our in-house 
e‑B2B app, is central 
to this transformation 
as it opens a direct 
communication channel 
with the universe of 
kiranas. With over 85% 
general trade business 
covered, Shikhar has 
fundamentally changed 
how kiranas operate by 
providing 24/7 ordering, 
transparent pricing, and 
credit solutions. 
~2 lakh 
new stores 
The consumer purchase journey in India has evolved 
into a distinctly omni-channel experience, seamlessly 
blending offline and online touchpoints as shoppers 
discover products at local kiranas, research on 
e-commerce platforms, and ultimately purchase through 
a mix of digital and physical channels. 
This rapid transformation is propelled by new retail 
formats, digital innovation, and shifting consumer 
preferences. With access to over 9 million retail outlets 
nationwide, we are uniquely positioned to integrate 
general trade, modern trade, e-commerce, and quick 
commerce channels, and are focused on enhancing 
our brands’ accessibility wherever consumers shop. By 
investing in technology, expanding our reach, increasing 
channel availability, and forging strategic partnerships, 
we are deepening market penetration, shaping the future 
of consumer distribution, and unlocking new growth 
opportunities in an increasingly dynamic marketplace. 
We are deeply focused on unlocking capital for our 
general trade distributors to ensure the entire ecosystem 
remains agile and profitable. We are achieving this by 
simplifying reverse logistics, unlocking slow-moving 
portfolios from our distributors, and implementing a 
robust, data-driven approach to inventory management. 
By helping our partners to hold the ‘right inventory’ 
through optimal, higher-frequency replenishment 
models, we are optimising the working capital deployed 
across the value chain.
We continue to view general trade as the bedrock of our 
distribution network. Our core strategy follows a ‘kirana-
centric, distributor-inclusive’ model, where we act as 
partners in the digital journey of millions of neighbourhood 
retailers. We are aggressively driving coverage expansion 
into the rural hinterlands to ensure our brands are 
“unmissably available” to every consumer. We have added 
~2 lakh new stores to our reach during the year.
Strengthening General Trade

Quick Commerce: Investing 
to Create Future Moats
Quick commerce is rapidly redefining the retail landscape 
in India by delivering essential and premium products in 
short timespan, meeting rising consumer expectations for 
speed and convenience. This immediacy boosts consumer 
satisfaction and loyalty, opening new consumption 
occasions. The channel doubled its turnover in FY 2025-26 
and has emerged as a vital growth engine for the Company.
Succeeding on quick commerce demands real-time 
inventory management and agile replenishment systems. 
Consumer behaviour is need-driven with purchase 
decisions made in seconds, making visibility and  
relevance critical. To navigate this evolving channel, 
we have established a dedicated quick commerce 
organisation. This business unit is empowered to drive  
end-to-end execution, streamlining processes, accelerating 
innovation, and ensuring seamless collaboration. We are 
also leveraging data-driven insights to curate the right 
assortment and maintain high availability. Our strategy 
focuses on superior agility; by partnering with the supply 
chain verticals of quick commerce players, we react to 
market spikes in real-time, ensuring ‘dark stores’ and hubs 
never run out of essential stocks. 
As a result of our 
dedicated actions, 
during the year, quick 
commerce service 
levels have increased 
by 1400 bps. 
We are transforming digital engagement through 
meticulously crafted content, designed to convert 
consumer interest into action. At the heart of this 
transformation is our industry-leading AI-enable pre-
testing engine, which rigourously evaluates all marketing 
assets before release. This ‘digital rehearsal’ ensures that 
advertisements and videos are informative, engaging, and 
optimised for maximum impact, allowing only the most 
effective content to reach consumers.
Beyond elevating content quality, we are advancing 
analytics capabilities by shifting from retrospective 
reporting to ‘Root Cause Prescriptive Analytics’. Instead of 
simply analysing past outcomes, our sophisticated data 
stack diagnoses the underlying drivers of market trends 
and prescribes targeted actions to enhance execution at 
the last mile. This enables us to anticipate market needs 
and ensure consistent product availability at the right 
locations and moments.
We are also accelerating our performance marketing 
initiatives by leveraging AI-enabled algorithms and 
strategic technology partnerships. Our focus has evolved 
from generic messaging to hyper-optimised, platform-
specific customisations, addressing unique shopper needs 
in real time and guiding them seamlessly from digital 
discovery to purchase. Simultaneously, we are upgrading 
offline execution with innovative tools like Envision, an 
image-recognition solution that detects out-of-stock items 
in priority stores, triggering immediate replenishment.
By integrating operational agility, real-time data insights, 
and advanced tools across online and offline channels, 
we are building a responsive retail ecosystem. This 
holistic strategy meets consumer needs, secures market 
leadership, and delivers meaningful value in a rapidly 
changing environment.

Our responsible sourcing approach is focused on 
building resilient, sustainable, and future-ready value 
chains. As input volatility, climate risks, and regulatory 
expectations continue to evolve, our approach has 
expanded from compliance-led sourcing to value-driven 
ecosystem collaboration. 
We are strengthening sustainability at source through 
integrated sourcing models, backward integration, 
and regenerative practices. In palm-based inputs, we 
continue to embed No Deforestation, No Peat principles 
in our sourcing. This is complemented by deeper supplier 
partnerships aimed at improving traceability, reducing 
environmental footprint, and enhancing long-term 
supply security.

A Commitment to Planet and People
We are driving transformation in our ecosystem by forging 
deep ‘Partnerships for Growth’ to unlock mutual value, 
fostering a ‘Play to Win’ mindset within our people to 
ensure high accountability, and institutionalising an  
AI-age supply chain that leverages advanced analytics 
and digital twins to move from local to global maxima. 
Building on these shifts, our strategic priorities are 
focused on delivering agility with discipline, driving 
productivity and cost competitiveness, strengthening 
cash delivery while improving asset efficiency across 
the value chain and advancing a segmented supply 
chain that enables differentiated service models across

foundations.

At HUL, our supply chain is undergoing a fundamental 
transformation from a scale-driven model to a 
distributed, adaptive and technology-enabled 
manufacturing network that bridges the manufacturing 
scale to meet personalised consumer needs.
In a country as vast as India, speed is our greatest advantage. 
Through strategic initiatives, we are decentralising our 
manufacturing footprint. This structural shift enhances 
service agility by reducing lead time, lowering costs and 
enabling leaner inventory.
Manufacturing innovations 
for the future
Technology is deeply integrated at the core of our 
operations. With AI-powered loss-capture tools, 
advanced statistical process controls combined with 
an AI-driven intelligent layer we are enabling efficient 
scheduling, real-time performance monitoring, and 
advanced automation. 
Digital is No Longer a Layer — It is 
Core to How we run Our Network
Supply Chain for Quick Commerce

while investing in ecosystem capabilities to scale recycled 
content, particularly in flexible plastics, and enable long-
term circular packaging solutions. 
Our Responsible Partner Policy (RPP) continues to guide 
engagement with suppliers, anchored in business integrity, 
human rights, and environmental stewardship. Rather than 
a prescriptive approach, we adopt a mutual recognition 
model, partnering with suppliers who demonstrate 
equivalent or stronger sustainability standards, supported 
by robust due diligence and independent verification. 
Responsible sourcing will remain a key lever in 
strengthening supply resilience, unlocking efficiencies 
through digital traceability and data-led insights, and 
building long-term competitive advantage for HUL.
To fuel our 
‘Accelerating Bigger, 
Bolder Innovations’ 
(ABBI) agenda, we 
are deploying global-
first technologies like 
‘Adaptive Nano’. 
This allows us to manufacture flexible batch sizes 
with faster changeovers, and multi-format capability, 
significantly improving responsiveness while sharply 
cutting down our innovation timelines. Similarly, we are 
integrating ‘Intelligent Mixers’ technology with high-speed 
automated packing, that materially improves our capex 
efficiency, and benefits formats like the Home Care liquids.
This progress is reflected in global recognition of our 
manufacturing and digital capabilities. During the year, 
two additional sites Gandhidham and Puducherry were 
recognised by the World Economic Forum as Advanced 
Fourth Industrial Revolution (4IR) Lighthouse factories.
HUL now houses five 
Lighthouse sites.
We are building a 
fully integrated, 
end-to-end digital 
ecosystem, connecting 
planning, sourcing, 
manufacturing, and 
distribution into a single, 
cohesive system. 
Our fulfilment model is designed to operate as part of a 
single, integrated supply chain with our customers, and 
where planning, manufacturing, and distribution work in 
sync to serve demand at speed. 
As the first step HUL implemented real-time demand capture 
systems, supported by self-healing master data management 
and collaborative forecasting models. By integrating 
customer signals fully in the process, we have significantly 
improved forecast accuracy for quick commerce partners. 
To maximise in-stock performance at customer fulfilment 
centres, we strengthened availability through smart planning, 
dynamic prioritisation and allocation models that guarantee 
fast-moving SKUs get timely replenishment. 
Our fulfilment design is tailored for speed and flexibility. Our 
segmented delivery model with agile fleet design ensures 
dynamic prioritisation and more frequent fulfilment. 
We redesigned distribution centre operations with dedicated 
picking lanes and express dock allocation enabling daily 
dispatch governance. Collaborative initiatives with customers 
such as single-click GRN, barcode-based automation, and 
shared visibility are further reducing turnaround time and 
driving operational excellence. 
These interventions have shown encouraging results, leading 
to 1400 bps improvements in customer service levels and in-
stock performance in this high-growth channel.
Together, these elements enable agile, resilient, and future-
ready, supply chain, positioning HUL to consistently deliver 
superior service while sustaining cost and operational 
excellence in an increasingly dynamic marketplace.
Digital transformation is the backbone of HUL’s supply 
chain evolution, enabling the transition from reactive 
operations to predictive, intelligent, and increasingly 
autonomous systems.
Digital Transformation
At the centre of this transformation is ‘Samarth’, our Supply 
Chain Nerve Centre— a unified intelligence layer with over 
90 terabytes of data at the foundation, integrated with 
30+ AI solutions, digital twins, and advanced analytics to 
enable predictive, self-healing operations. 
Planning is evolving into a cognitive, no touch system, with 
AI-driven demand sensing and synchronised production 
scheduling to accelerate decision making. 
Manufacturing is progressing towards autonomous  
‘lights-off’ operations, with ~15% of volumes in key 
pack-formats already running through dark operations, 
supported by AI- powered controls, real-time optimisation 
and advanced process controls.
Across distribution and fulfilment, EDI enabled 
collaboration, IoT enabled distribution centres, intelligent 
allocation engines and automated logistics are driving 
faster, more accurate service. 
In sourcing, advanced analytics and ML models are 
strengthening cost competitiveness and supplier decision 
making.
Together, these capabilities are enabling an integrated, 
connected, self-learning supply chain, with increasing 
levels of autonomous execution.
Quick commerce represents one of the most significant shifts 
in the FMCG landscape, characterised by rapid growth, high 
volatility, and near-instant fulfilment expectations.
For HUL, winning in quick commerce requires a supply chain 
that is hyper responsive and digitally intelligent. Our bespoke 
quick commerce supply chain design is built to deliver 
superior availability, rapid fulfilment, and strong category 
presence across dark stores operating in highly volatile, 
tightly scheduled environments.

HUL is unwavering in its commitment to health, safety, 
and wellbeing for all our employees and partners 
across the value chain. Our approach has evolved to 
prioritise prevention of unsafe acts, digitally-enabled

to Operate’ now offers real-time visibility to factory 
employees’ skill levels, supporting strict adherence to our 
Life Critical Standards and 10 Safety Life Saving Pledges. 
Enhanced governance integrates site-level safety data, 
ensuring transparency and data-led decisions.
We are dedicated to creating a workplace where 
employees can thrive and are supported by holistic 
programmes that nurture their physical, mental, and 
emotional wellbeing. By investing in comprehensive 
support systems, we foster a culture of psychological safety 
that empowers individuals to seek help with confidence.
All-in for Safety
Wellbeing at Work
At HUL, our people remain at the heart of everything 
we do. Their passion, creativity, and resilience 
power our ability to deliver enduring value to all 
our stakeholders. We continue to invest in attracting, 
developing, and retaining outstanding talent, 
recognising that their growth fuels the growth of our 
organisation. By fostering a culture grounded in high 
performance, inclusion and psychological safety, we 
enable our teams to thrive, innovate, and realise their 
full potential.
Our six pillar road safety 
strategy, ‘Drive to Zero’, 
strengthens defensive 
driving across our transport 
network. Our Advanced Driver 
Assistance Systems (ADAS) and 
Driver Management Centres 
(DMCs) under the ‘Suraksha 
Sabke Saath’ initiative use 
telematics to deliver targeted 
behavioural interventions, 
recognising unsafe actions 
and reinforcing safe driving 
habits in real time. These 
efforts foster a culture of 
responsibility and care in our 
extended value chain.
Our flagship ‘Healthier U’ 
programme has delivered 
a 5% improvement 
in health outcomes 
over the past three 
years, demonstrating 
the tangible impact 
of preventive health 
initiatives. The programme 
includes health risk 
assessments, wellness 
drives, onsite medical 
check-ups, vaccination 
campaigns, eye care 
camps, and mental health 
support systems. 
In parallel, we continue to strengthen the mental health 
and wellbeing resources offered to our people. Our 
trained Mental Health Champions (MHCs) are equipped 
to offer peer-to-peer support and guidance. Our Global 
Health & Wellbeing Hub further enhances accessibility 
by connecting employees directly to MHCs, offering 
e-learning modules for managers, and providing self-
learning resources. Additionally, our 24x7 Employee 
Assistance Programme (EAP) ensures confidential 
counselling and support whenever needed.

HUL continues to be recognised as the #1 Employer of Choice

Alongside attracting the best talent, we continue to retain

Future-fit Workforce
Fostering Inclusivity
With shifting market dynamics, evolving consumer 
expectations, and emerging technologies, building 
a future-ready workforce is critical. At HUL, we are 
proactively investing in capability development, both 
Leadership and Functional, to ensure our people can 
embrace change while stepping up effectiveness.
At HUL, fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace is not

culture, policies, and everyday actions. 
Our progressive and equitable people policies reinforce 
inclusion across the organisation, reflected in the 84%# 
of employees who believe HUL provides an environment 
where people of diverse backgrounds can thrive. With 
more than 20 equitable policies, we support employees 
across their life stages and lived realities, from flexible

We have significantly strengthened frontline marketing 
and sales capabilities through targeted digital 
interventions, while accelerating upskilling across our 
shop-floor workforce. 50% of shop-floor employees are

HUL’s flagship ‘Skillup’ programme continues to transform 
customer development frontline talent. Curated and

Strengthening our Distinctive Edge: 
Attracting & Retaining Superior 
Talent 
By leveraging technology, we have invested in social-first 
employer brand initiatives and recruitment marketing 
events, both digital and physical. This is further supported 
by a 100+ member employee advocacy community to 
amplify our reach across platforms. This ensures an always 
engaged external talent community, who are potential 
candidates for future roles.
Beyond campus hiring, we have also diversified our 
mid-career recruitment strategy with targeted outreach 
to niche talent pools. To bridge skill and exposure gaps, 
we are building communities across critical future 
cohorts. Our approach is to strengthen our ‘Employee 
Value Proposition’ externally with targeted recruitment 
marketing to attract managerial and senior leadership 
talent in high demand domains. Additionally, we are

Our annual employee 
survey UniVoice saw 
83% participation from 
office-based employees,

opportunities for

commitment to

bps year-on-year.

Through transformational programmes like ‘Ahilya’,

The unwavering commitment towards these actions

We remain steadfast in our journey towards becoming

section_title: Planet & Society
chunk:
Planet & Society
We are also advancing logistics decarbonisation through 
our “Load more, travel less” approach and by scaling LNG/
CNG and EV-based distribution.
Scope 3 emissions remain a challenge due to the 
significant contribution of petrochemical ingredients 
and end-of-life emissions, particularly within the Home 
Care portfolio. To address this, we are deepening supplier 
engagement, advancing low carbon material pathways 
and supporting policy advocacy that accelerates system-
wide transitions.
Through our Supplier Climate Programme, we are working 
with key suppliers to develop costed decarbonisation 
plans while strengthening measurement, reporting and 
reduction efforts across the value chain. We are advancing 
low Greenhouse gas (GHG) soda ash procurement 
in partnership with a key supplier, and reformulating 
ingredients in soaps, by reducing Total Fatty Matter (TFM) 
and high GHG palm oil with environmentally sustainable 
Palm (NDP palm), while maintaining performance, 
affordability and consumer experience.
We continue to drive climate action beyond our value 
chain through strategic collaborations. Through the 
G20 Resource Efficiency Circular Economy Industry 
Coalition (RECEIC), we are helping accelerate the 
chemical sector’s transition to bio-based and renewable 
alternatives, supported by a knowledge paper outlining 
the technology, infrastructure and policy shifts needed 
for scale. We are also backing the tea sector’s Net Zero 
transition through research and capability building and 
have launched the Federation of Indian Chambers of 
Commerce and Industry (FICCI) Centre for Sustainability 
Leadership to strengthen SME capabilities nationwide. 
To accelerate climate positive innovation, we have set 
up a Climate Incubation Hub in partnership with NSRCEL 
(IIM Bangalore) to test and scale next-generation low 
carbon technologies across ingredients, materials, 
energy systems and waste-to-value pathways, 
advancing system level transformation.
Climate
Nature
Sustainability is central to how we create long-term value.  
We have sharpened our agenda with clear near and medium-
term goals across four priority areas where we can deliver 
the greatest impact - Climate, Nature, Plastics, and Livelihoods, 
underpinned by a strong commitment to Human Rights. This 
focused approach enables us to stay agile and respond to 
evolving expectations from consumers, regulators and society.
We are scaling proven models across these priorities to drive 
greater impact with our actions contributing to national 
development priorities while advancing Unilever’s global 
sustainability ambitions.
Introduction
Climate change poses a material risk to our business 
making decarbonisation a strategic priority. We are 
focused on reducing Scope 1 and 2 emissions while 
accelerating progress on Scope 3, which constitutes the 
largest share of our footprint. 
Across our operations, we are phasing out fossil 
fuels and transitioning to renewable energy. All our 
manufacturing sites are now coal-free, and 100% of our 
electricity is sourced from renewable sources through 
a mix of solar, wind and IREC certified green power. 
Energy efficiency and site upgrades continue to lower 
our overall GHG footprint.
Our business depends on resilient ecosystems, and 
we are committed to protecting and restoring natural 
resources across our value chain. Our ambition is to build 
regenerative ecosystems while ensuring deforestation-
free sourcing.
We continue to strengthen sustainable sourcing across 12 key 
crops and advance regenerative agriculture that improves 
soil health, biodiversity and long-term land productivity. 
Tea remains a priority crop: we procure Trustea-certified 
tea and work closely with smallholders to enhance 
sustainable practices. Through a Partnership Growth Charter 
with Trustea, we are expanding regenerative practices, 
strengthening safe pesticide management and improving 
market access; and through TeaNext in Assam, we have 
trained 4,892 small tea growers in regenerative agriculture 
and Maximum Residue Limit (MRL) compliance.
Our factories continue to demonstrate 
global leadership in sustainable, digitally 
enabled operations. HUL is the only FMCG 
company in India with five WEF-recognised 
4IR Lighthouse sites, showcasing advanced use 
of AI, automation, digital twins and industrial 
internet of things (IIoT) to boost productivity 
and environmental performance. Our Mumbai 
headquarters is LEED Platinum certified, and 
we are strengthening climate and water 
stewardship across sites. Sumerpur has 
achieved IGBC Platinum as a carbon-neutral, 
solar-powered, zero-water-waste factory, 
while Chhindwara and Hosur have earned 
Alliance for Water Stewardship (AWS) Platinum 
award for responsible water management.
Stakeholder Engagement and Review
75
74
HINDUSTAN UNILEVER LIMITED
INTEGRATED ANNUAL REPORT 2025-26


Our livelihoods agenda focuses on enhancing lives 
across our value chain, improving incomes, building 
economic resilience and strengthening our business. 
We work to raise living standards not only for those 
directly connected to our value chain but also for the 
communities we operate in. Our approach is rooted in 
implementation, partnerships and collective action.
Our Project Shakti continues to empower over 200,000 
rural women by providing entrepreneurship training that 
enhances livelihoods and supports long-term financial 
independence. 
To strengthen India’s retail and frontline sales value chain, 
SAFAL—our flagship livelihoods programme, enhances 
employment, employability and entrepreneurship among 
women, youth and Persons with Disabilities in the sales 
and retail sector. Through SAFAL, and in partnership with 
the Ministry of Skill Development and Entrepreneurship, 
the curriculum is now available as a digital public good 
on the Skill India Digital Hub, making HUL the first FMCG 
company onboarded.
Our community development programme, Prabhat, 
operates livelihood centres that train women and youth 
in vocational skills (IT, electrical work, plumbing, tailoring 
and solar) and has benefited over 12 million individuals 
through its broader community interventions.
As part of Project Circular Bharat, we collaborate with 
UNDP enabling Safai Saathis to transition from the 
informal sector into the mainstream workforce. To date, 
the programme has reached 35,000 waste workers, 
expanding access to social protection, improving 
working conditions and creating pathways for formal 
livelihoods.
Livelihoods
Guided by Unilever’s Regenerative Agriculture Principles, 
we work with farmers to restore soil health, capture carbon 
and regenerate degraded land through capacity building 
and field engagement. In 2025, we accelerated these efforts 
across sourcing regions, from tea gardens in Assam to dairy 
communities in Northern India, supporting upliftment through 
better practices and improved market linkages.
AWANI, our flagship biodiversity initiative aligned with 
India’s National Biodiversity Strategy and Action Plan, 
addresses biodiversity loss, water scarcity and land 
degradation through habitat restoration, sustainable forest 
management and inclusive land use. It also strengthens 
forest dependent livelihoods, reduces human–wildlife 
conflict and builds anti-poaching and anti-grazing 
capacities across the Indian Himalayan Region, the 
Sundarbans and the Western Ghats.
Water stewardship is central to our nature agenda. We 
are addressing shared water challenges across 15 water 
stressed factory sites, and our Chhindwara and Hosur 
sites have achieved AWS Platinum for responsible water 
management. 
Plastics
Our plastic packaging strategy focuses on the areas 
across our value chain where we can deliver the greatest 
impact, accelerating the shift from a linear take-make-
dispose model to a circular economy where materials 
remain at their highest value for as long as possible.
We are redesigning products and packaging to cut virgin 
plastic and increase Post consumer recycled (PCR) plastic 
usage. Today, more than 25 HUL brands incorporate 
PCR in their packaging. We continue to work with Lucro 
Plastecycle to strengthen flexible plastics circularity, to 
scale high quality recycled flexible plastics in India.
Through Project Circular Bharat (PCB), we are 
operationalising an end-to-end circularity model that 
integrates behaviour change for source segregation, 
social inclusion of waste workers and effective recycling 
infrastructure. During the year, PCB scaled to 10+ cities, 
working with 25+ partners including government, 
creating the potential to divert over 100,000 tonnes of 
waste from landfills annually. Over the next three years, 
HUL will also support 50 circularity startups in partnership 
with NITI Aayog’s Atal Innovation Mission (AIM) prioritising 
plastic circularity and next generation materials.
Through the Hindustan 
Unilever Foundation 
(HUF), our not-for-profit 
subsidiary, we continue 
to scale water security 
solutions, cumulatively 
creating over 4.5 trillion 
litres of water potential 
while strengthening 
community resilience 
and climate adaptation.
For more, visit www.hul.co.in or refer to our CSR and 
BRSR Reports
Together, our actions across Climate, Nature, Plastics and 
Livelihoods reflect a focused commitment to delivering 
meaningful sustainability impact while strengthening 
business resilience. While challenges remain, we are 
making steady, purposeful progress, contributing to 
India’s sustainable development priorities and creating 
long-term value for our people, partners and the planet.
Stakeholder Engagement and Review
